sourceName: Tourism, Hospitality and Service Management Resume
resumeLength: One page with service, operations, language, and event evidence easy to scan.

formattingRules.fontRecommendation: Polished professional font; warm but not decorative.
formattingRules.bulletStyle: Customer/service context first, then scale and reliability outcome.

writingRules.tone: Professional, service-oriented, cross-cultural, and operationally reliable.
writingRules.bulletFormula: Action + service/operations context + guest/customer/stakeholder group + scale + satisfaction, efficiency, or reliability outcome
quantificationRules: Prefer concrete scope: sample size, stakeholder count, case volume, event size, model accuracy, project duration, budget, risk, or response time when supported by source facts.
When exact numbers are absent, use bounded evidence such as tool, method, deliverable, audience, or process scope; never invent metrics.
Use metrics differently by domain: technical performance for engineering, compliance/accuracy for audit, writing/research output for law and policy, service reliability for hospitality.

exampleBullets.strong: Coordinated guest check-in and vendor communication for campus events, improving handoff clarity across operations volunteers.
exampleBullets.reason: Shows service process, stakeholder group, and reliability value.

exampleBullets.strong: Resolved visitor inquiries in English and Mandarin while maintaining accurate booking and service records.
exampleBullets.reason: Connects customer service to language ability and operational accuracy.

scoringCriteria.description: Conventional headings, readable layout, aligned dates, consistent spacing, and parser-friendly text.
Prominent sections and bullets match the target role and market context.
Bullets show action, method, scope, and outcome without unsupported claims.
Specific, concise, role-native language with no inflated AI phrasing.
Structure and density match the selected role, country, and CV/resume style.

promptSnippets.title: Tourism, Hospitality and Service Management Resume routing rule
promptSnippets.content: When the target role matches Tourism / Hospitality / Service Management, use Tourism, Hospitality and Service Management Resume as the primary industry route and adopt this formula: Action + service/operations context + guest/customer/stakeholder group + scale + satisfaction, efficiency, or reliability outcome.

promptSnippets.title: Tourism, Hospitality and Service Management Resume language guardrail
promptSnippets.content: Preserve domain-native evidence for Tourism / Hospitality / Service Management; do not convert the profile into a generic tech, finance, or consulting resume unless the user target explicitly requires it.

sourceReferences.label: Local 123456 CV sample corpus
sourceReferences.note: 56 newly added CV/resume samples from docs/sample-resume-knowledge; abstracted into rules only, without copying resume text.

sourceReferences.label: Existing local resume-learning corpus
